docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The BBG Rebate Processing Tool is an internal automation system designed to transform quarterly rebate Excel files into TradeNet-ready CSV files.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
This tool processes Excel rebate files (.xlsm format) from BBG members and:

  • Validates and extracts rebate data from quarterly usage reporting sheets
  • Enriches the data with TradeNet member and supplier information
  • Applies business rules to map supplier names correctly
  • Generates clean CSV files formatted exactly to TradeNet's import specifications

docs.title: Dual Format Support
docs.text: The tool automatically detects and processes both OLD and NEW Excel formats: - NEW Format: Member ID is present in cell B6 - OLD Format: Member ID is blank in B6, looked up by member name

docs.title: Fast Processing
docs.text:
• Single files process in seconds
  • Batch mode can process up to 50 files at once
  • Outputs either merged CSV or ZIP archive of individual files

docs.title: Smart Data Enrichment
docs.text:
• Automatically looks up BBG Member IDs from the TradeNet Members directory
  • Maps supplier names to TradeNet Supplier IDs
  • Applies configurable business rules for special supplier mappings

docs.title: Flexible Output Options
docs.text:
• Single File Mode: Process one file, get one CSV
  • Batch Mode - Merged: Process multiple files, get one combined CSV
  • Batch Mode - ZIP: Process multiple files, get individual CSVs in a ZIP archive

docs.title: Business Rules Engine
docs.text: Create and manage custom rules to handle special cases: - Map specific supplier names to correct TradeNet suppliers - Override supplier mappings based on product IDs - Enable/disable rules without deleting them - Rules are applied in priority order

docs.title: Lookup Management
docs.text: Maintain the reference data that powers the tool: - Members Directory: 900+ BBG member companies with their TradeNet IDs - Suppliers Directory: 90+ suppliers with their TradeNet IDs - Upload new CSV files to replace directories - Add or edit individual records through the interface

docs.title: Who Is This For?
docs.text: This tool is designed for the BBG internal team (3-4 staff members) who process quarterly rebate submissions from member companies. It requires no technical knowledge to use—just upload files and download the results.

docs.title: Output Format
docs.text:
The tool generates CSV files with exactly 15 columns matching TradeNet's import specification:

  1. member_name
  2. bbg_member_id
  3. confirmed_occupancy
  4. job_code
  5. address1
  6. city
  7. state
  8. zip_postal
  9. address_type
  10. quantity
  11. product_id
  12. supplier_name
  13. tradenet_supplier_id
  14. pp_dist_subcontractor
  15. tradenet_company_id

docs.title: Technology Stack
docs.text: For Technical Users: - Frontend: React, Vite, TailwindCSS (deployed on Render Static Site) - Backend: Python, FastAPI (deployed on Render Web Service) - Database: PostgreSQL on Render (SQLite for local development) - Processing: Python libraries (openpyxl, pandas)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
1. Open the tool in your web browser (URL provided by IT)
  2. Click or drag an Excel file to upload
  3. Click Process & Preview to see the results
  4. Review the preview data
  5. Click Download CSV to get your file
  6. Import the CSV into TradeNet

That's it! For detailed instructions, see the User Guide.

docs.title: POST /api/upload
docs.text:
Upload and process a single Excel file, returns preview data.

Request: 

POST /api/upload
Content-Type: multipart/form-data

file: [Excel file binary]

Parameters: - file (form-data, required): Excel file (.xlsm or .xlsx)

Response: 

{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "preview": [
      {
        "member_name": "Bosgraaf Homes",
        "bbg_member_id": "1003",
        "confirmed_occupancy": "7/2/25",
        ...
      }
    ],
    "total_rows": 1380,
    "member_name": "Bosgraaf Homes",
    "bbg_member_id": "1003"
  }
}

Status Codes: - 200: Success - 400: Invalid file type or size - 500: Processing error

docs.title: Python (requests)
docs.text:
import requests

# Upload file
with open('file.xlsm', 'rb') as f:
    files = {'file': f}
    response = requests.post('http://localhost:8001/api/upload', files=files)
    data = response.json()
    print(f"Processed {data['data']['total_rows']} rows")

# Get rules
response = requests.get('http://localhost:8001/api/rules')
rules = response.json()
print(f"Found {len(rules)} rules")

docs.title: Database Schema
docs.text: Database structure and schema documentation for the BBG Rebate Processing Tool.

docs.title: Database Overview
docs.text: Type: PostgreSQL (Production on Render) / SQLite (Local Development) Production: PostgreSQL on Render.com Local Development: backend/bbg_rebates.db (SQLite) ORM: SQLAlchemy 2.0+ Size: ~500KB (with seed data, SQLite) / Variable (PostgreSQL)

docs.title: 1. lookup_members
docs.text:
TradeNet Members directory

Column Type Constraints Description id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, AUTO INCREMENT Unique member ID tradenet_company_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, INDEXED TradeNet company ID bbg_member_id VARCHAR(100) INDEXED BBG member ID (can be empty) member_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, INDEXED Company name last_updated DATETIME DEFAULT NOW Last update timestamp

Indexes: - ix_lookup_members_tradenet_company_id - ix_lookup_members_bbg_member_id - ix_lookup_members_member_name

Typical Row Count: ~911 members

docs.title: 2. lookup_suppliers
docs.text:
TradeNet Suppliers directory

Column Type Constraints Description id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, AUTO INCREMENT Unique supplier ID tradenet_supplier_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, UNIQUE, INDEXED TradeNet supplier ID bbg_id VARCHAR(100) INDEXED Buying Group ID (optional) supplier_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, INDEXED Supplier company name active_flag INTEGER NULL Active status (0 or 1) contact_info TEXT NULL Contact information last_updated DATETIME DEFAULT NOW Last update timestamp

Indexes: - ix_lookup_suppliers_tradenet_supplier_id (unique) - ix_lookup_suppliers_bbg_id - ix_lookup_suppliers_supplier_name

Typical Row Count: ~91 suppliers

docs.title: 3. lookup_products
docs.text:
Programs & Products (currently unused in processing)

Column Type Constraints Description id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, AUTO INCREMENT Unique product ID program_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, INDEXED Program identifier program_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL Program name product_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, INDEXED Product identifier product_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL Product name proof_point VARCHAR(255) NULL Proof point description last_updated DATETIME DEFAULT NOW Last update timestamp

Note: Products are currently extracted from uploaded files, not from this table.

docs.title: 4. rules
docs.text:
Business rules engine

Column Type Constraints Description id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, AUTO INCREMENT Unique rule ID name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL Human-readable rule name rule_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL Type of rule (see types below) priority INTEGER NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, INDEXED Execution order (lower = first) enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL, DEFAULT TRUE Whether rule is active config JSON NOT NULL Rule configuration (structure varies by type) created_at DATETIME NOT NULL, DEFAULT NOW Creation timestamp updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL, DEFAULT NOW Last update timestamp

Rule Types: - search_replace - Find and replace text - if_then_update - Conditional field update - if_then_set - Conditional value setting - row_filter - Filter rows by condition - concatenate - Combine multiple fields

Config Examples:

// search_replace
{
  "column": "supplier_name",
  "find": "Day & Night Heating & Cooling",
  "replace": "Carrier"
}

// if_then_update (simple)
{
  "condition": {
    "supplier_name_equals": "Day & Night"
  },
  "set_supplier": "Carrier"
}

// if_then_update (complex)
{
  "condition": {
    "logic": "AND",
    "rules": [
      {"field": "product_id", "operator": "contains", "value": "5534"},
      {"field": "quantity", "operator": ">", "value": 0}
    ]
  },
  "then_action": {
    "field": "supplier_name",
    "value": "Air Vent"
  }
}

Typical Row Count: 8-20 rules

docs.title: 5. processed_files (Cached Files)
docs.text:
Temporarily cached processed files

Column Type Constraints Description id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, AUTO INCREMENT Unique cache ID job_id VARCHAR(36) NOT NULL, UNIQUE, INDEXED UUID for job filename VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL Original filename file_data BLOB NOT NULL Processed CSV/ZIP data file_type VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL "csv" or "zip" row_count INTEGER NULL Number of rows processed file_count INTEGER NULL Number of files (batch mode) status VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL "pending", "completed", "failed" created_at DATETIME NOT NULL, DEFAULT NOW Creation timestamp expires_at DATETIME NOT NULL Expiration timestamp

Indexes: - ix_processed_files_job_id (unique) - ix_processed_files_status - ix_processed_files_created_at

Typical Row Count: 0-100 (auto-cleaned after expiration)

docs.title: Entity Relationships
docs.text:
┌──────────────────────┐
│  lookup_members      │
│  (911 records)       │
└──────────────────────┘
         │
         │ (Used by data enricher)
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  File Processing Pipeline           │
│  (In-memory, no DB persistence)     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
         │
         │ (Applies rules)
         ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│  rules               │
│  (8-20 records)      │
│  - priority ordered  │
└──────────────────────┘
         │
         │ (Looks up supplier IDs)
         ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│  lookup_suppliers    │
│  (91 records)        │
└──────────────────────┘
         │
         │ (Optional caching)
         ▼
┌──────────────────────┐
│  processed_files     │
│  (temporary cache)   │
└──────────────────────┘

Note: No foreign key relationships. Tables are independent lookup directories.

docs.title: Automatic Creation
docs.text:
Tables are created automatically on first run via SQLAlchemy:

# In backend/app/database.py
from app.models.lookup import TradeNetMember, Supplier, ProgramProduct
from app.models.rule import Rule
from app.models.processed_file import ProcessedFile

# Tables created when engine starts
Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)

docs.title: Common Queries
docs.text:
Get all enabled rules by priority: 

SELECT * FROM rules
WHERE enabled = 1
ORDER BY priority ASC;

Find member by name: 

SELECT * FROM lookup_members
WHERE member_name = 'Bosgraaf Homes';

Find supplier by name: 

SELECT * FROM lookup_suppliers
WHERE supplier_name = 'Carrier';

Count records: 

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM lookup_members;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM lookup_suppliers;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM rules WHERE enabled = 1;

docs.title: Indexes
docs.text: All frequently-queried columns are indexed: - Member names (for OLD format lookups) - Supplier names (for rule application) - IDs (for joins and lookups) - Rule priority (for ordered execution)

docs.title: Query Performance
docs.text:
• Member lookup: < 1ms (indexed search)
  • Supplier lookup: < 1ms (indexed search)
  • Rules fetch: < 5ms (small table, indexed priority)

docs.title: Current Production Setup
docs.text:
PostgreSQL on Render: - ✅ Multiple concurrent connections - ✅ Better write performance - ✅ Automatic backups by Render - ✅ Production-grade reliability

Local Development (SQLite): - Multiple readers OK - Only ONE writer at a time - Write operations lock entire database - Sufficient for solo development

Note: Production is already using PostgreSQL on Render for better concurrency and reliability.

docs.title: Deployment Guide
docs.text: Guide for deploying the BBG Rebate Processing Tool to production.

docs.title: Deployment Overview
docs.text: Current Production Stack: - Backend: Render.com Web Service ✅ DEPLOYED - Frontend: Render.com Static Site ✅ DEPLOYED - Database: PostgreSQL on Render ✅ DEPLOYED - Documentation: GitHub Pages (optional)

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• GitHub account with repository access
  • Render.com account ✅ (you already have this)
  • Domain name (optional)

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Backend for Production
docs.text:
1.1 Update requirements.txt

Add production dependencies: 

cd backend
echo "psycopg2-binary==2.9.9" >> requirements.txt  # If using PostgreSQL
echo "gunicorn==21.2.0" >> requirements.txt

1.2 Create Render configuration

Create render.yaml in project root: 

services:
  - type: web
    name: bbg-rebate-api
    env: python
    buildCommand: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt
    startCommand: cd backend && gunicorn app.main:app -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker --bind 0.0.0.0:$PORT
    envVars:
      - key: PYTHON_VERSION
        value: 3.12.0
      - key: DATABASE_URL
        fromDatabase:
          name: bbg-rebates-db
          property: connectionString

1.3 Update CORS settings

Edit backend/app/main.py: 

# Add your Render Static Site URL to allowed origins
allow_origins=[
    "http://localhost:5173",
    "http://localhost:5174",
    "https://your-frontend.onrender.com",  # Your Render Static Site URL
    "https://your-custom-domain.com",  # Add if using custom domain
]

docs.title: Step 2: Deploy to Render
docs.text:
2.1 Create New Web Service 1. Go to https://render.com/dashboard 2. Click "New +" → "Web Service" 3. Connect your GitHub repository 4. Configure: - Name: bbg-rebate-api - Environment: Python 3 - Build Command: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt - Start Command: cd backend && gunicorn app.main:app -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker --bind 0.0.0.0:$PORT - Plan: Free or Starter ($7/mo)

2.2 Add Environment Variables - DATABASE_URL - PostgreSQL connection string (if using PostgreSQL) - ALLOWED_ORIGINS - Your frontend URL

2.3 Add PostgreSQL Database ✅ COMPLETED 1. Click "New +" → "PostgreSQL" 2. Name: bbg-rebates-db 3. Link to web service 4. Connection string added to environment variables

Note: You are currently using PostgreSQL on Render, not SQLite with persistent disk.

# SSH into Render shell (if available on paid plan)
cd backend
python3 seed_real_data.py
python3 seed_rules.py

docs.title: File Upload & Processing Flow
docs.text:
1. USER UPLOADS FILE
   ↓
2. FileUpload.jsx (Frontend)
   - Validates file type (.xlsm, .xlsx)
   - Sends to backend via FormData
   ↓
3. /api/upload (Backend Router)
   - Receives multipart/form-data
   - Saves file temporarily
   ↓
4. Pipeline.process_file() (Service Layer)
   ↓
5. ExcelProcessor.read_file()
   - Opens Excel file with openpyxl
   - Detects OLD vs NEW format (check cell B6)
   - Extracts metadata (member info from B6/B7)
   - Extracts Programs-Products sheet
   - Reads Usage-Reporting data (rows 9+)
   ↓
6. DataTransformer.transform()
   - Unpivots wide format → long format
   - Formats dates (Excel → M/D/YY)
   - Converts numbers (remove decimals)
   - Standardizes column names
   ↓
7. DataEnricher.enrich()
   - Lookup member ID (if OLD format)
   - Apply business rules (supplier overrides)
   - Lookup supplier IDs from directory
   - Add TradeNet company IDs
   ↓
8. Generate CSV
   - Sort by date, job code, column order
   - Create 15-column CSV format
   ↓
9. Return to Frontend
   - Preview: JSON with first N rows
   - Download: CSV file blob
   ↓
10. Display or Download
    - Preview: DataPreview.jsx renders table
    - Download: Browser downloads CSV

docs.title: Business Rules Processing
docs.text:
1. Rule Definition (Database)
   - Priority order (1, 2, 3...)
   - Condition (field, operator, value)
   - Action (set field to value)
   ↓
2. Rule Loading
   - Load all ENABLED rules from database
   - Sort by priority (ascending)
   ↓
3. Rule Application (for each data row)
   - Iterate through rules in priority order
   - Check if condition matches row data
   - If match: apply action (modify field)
   - Continue to next rule
   ↓
4. Result
   - Modified data with rules applied
   - Higher priority rules can override lower ones

docs.title: 1. ExcelProcessor (app/services/excel_processor.py)
docs.text:
Purpose: Read and parse Excel files

Key Methods: - read_file(file_path) - Main entry point - _detect_format() - Check if OLD or NEW format - _extract_metadata() - Get member info from B6/B7 - _extract_products() - Read Programs-Products sheet - _read_data_rows() - Read Usage-Reporting rows

Important Logic: - Format Detection: If B6 is empty → OLD format - Product Identification: Row 2 flags (1 = active), Row 7 IDs - Data Start: Row 9 = headers, Row 10+ = data

docs.title: 2. DataTransformer (app/services/data_transformer.py)
docs.text:
Purpose: Transform wide Excel format to long CSV format

Key Methods: - transform(raw_data, products) - Main transformation - _unpivot() - Convert wide → long format - _format_dates() - Convert Excel dates - _format_numbers() - Remove decimals - _standardize_columns() - Ensure correct column names

Important Logic: - Unpivot: Each product column becomes multiple rows - Date Format: Excel serial → M/D/YY string - Numbers: quantity and zip_postal as integers

docs.title: 3. DataEnricher (app/services/data_enricher.py)
docs.text:
Purpose: Add lookup data and apply business rules

Key Methods: - enrich(data, lookups, rules) - Main enrichment - _lookup_member_id() - Find BBG member ID by name - _apply_rules() - Execute business rules in order - _lookup_supplier_ids() - Map supplier names to IDs

Important Logic: - Rules applied in priority order (1 first) - Each rule can override previous values - Lookups match on exact string equality

docs.title: 4. Pipeline (app/services/pipeline.py)
docs.text:
Purpose: Orchestrate the entire processing flow

Key Method: - process_file(file_path, db_session) - End-to-end processing

Flow: 1. Read Excel file 2. Transform data 3. Load lookups and rules from database 4. Enrich data 5. Generate CSV 6. Return result

docs.title: 1. UploadPage (src/pages/UploadPage.jsx)
docs.text:
Purpose: Main upload and processing page

State Management: - selectedFile - Single file in normal mode - selectedFiles - Multiple files in batch mode - batchMode - Toggle between single/batch - outputMode - Merged CSV or ZIP - previewData - Result data for preview - jobId - Cached processing ID for instant downloads

Key Functions: - handleProcess() - Process single file - handleBatchProcess() - Process multiple files - handleDownload() - Download single CSV - handleBatchDownload() - Download merged or ZIP

docs.title: 2. RulesManager (src/components/rules/RulesManager.jsx)
docs.text:
Purpose: Manage business rules

Features: - List all rules with priority - Toggle enable/disable - Edit existing rules - Delete rules - Add new rules (via AddRuleModal)

API Calls: - GET /api/rules - Fetch all rules - PUT /api/rules/{id} - Update rule - DELETE /api/rules/{id} - Delete rule - POST /api/rules - Create rule

docs.title: 3. LookupTablesPage (src/pages/LookupTablesPage.jsx)
docs.text:
Purpose: Manage Members and Suppliers directories

Features: - View members/suppliers tables - Add individual records - Edit records - Delete records - Upload CSV to replace entire table

docs.title: Why PostgreSQL in Production, SQLite in Development?
docs.text:
Decision: Use PostgreSQL on Render for production, SQLite for local development

Reasoning: - ✅ PostgreSQL (Production): - Better concurrency support - Production-grade reliability - Managed by Render (automatic backups) - Scales with growing user base - Better performance under load - ✅ SQLite (Development): - Simple local setup (no external DB server) - Single file database (easy to reset) - Faster development iteration - No configuration needed

Current Status: Deployed on Render with PostgreSQL database

docs.title: Why File-Based Product Extraction?
docs.text:
Decision: Extract products from each uploaded file instead of central database

Reasoning: - Each member has different active products - Product lists change per quarter - Avoids stale data in database - More dynamic and accurate

Original Plan: Load Programs-Products once into database Changed to: Extract from each file during processing

docs.title: Why FastAPI?
docs.text:
Decision: FastAPI over Flask/Django

Reasoning: - ✅ Built-in async support - ✅ Automatic API documentation (OpenAPI) - ✅ Fast performance - ✅ Type hints with Pydantic - ✅ Modern Python framework

docs.title: Why React (not Vue/Angular)?
docs.text:
Decision: React with functional components

Reasoning: - ✅ Large ecosystem - ✅ Easy to find developers - ✅ Component reusability - ✅ Hooks for state management (no Redux needed for this scale)

docs.title: Why Vite (not Create React App)?
docs.text:
Decision: Vite for build tooling

Reasoning: - ✅ Much faster dev server - ✅ Faster builds - ✅ Modern ESM-based - ✅ Better DX (developer experience)

docs.title: Why Material Theme for MkDocs?
docs.text:
Decision: MkDocs Material for documentation

Reasoning: - ✅ Professional appearance - ✅ Built-in search - ✅ Dark/light mode - ✅ Mobile responsive - ✅ Easy deployment to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Bottlenecks
docs.text:

docs.title: Potential Optimizations
docs.text:
If needed in future:

  1. Parallel Processing:
  2. Use multiprocessing for batch mode
  3. Process multiple files simultaneously

  4. Requires careful database transaction handling

  5. Caching:

  6. Cache lookup tables in memory (currently loaded each request)

  7. Use Redis for shared cache across processes

  8. Async Processing:

  9. Move file processing to background tasks (Celery, FastAPI BackgroundTasks)
  10. Return job ID immediately

  11. Poll for completion

  12. Faster Excel Reading:

  13. Try xlrd or pandas.read_excel() with engine='openpyxl'
  14. Benchmark different libraries

docs.title: What is the BBG Rebate Processing Tool?
docs.text: The BBG Rebate Processing Tool is an internal automation system that converts quarterly rebate Excel files from BBG members into TradeNet-ready CSV files. It reduces processing time from 3 weeks to under 2 minutes.

docs.title: Who can use this tool?
docs.text: This tool is for BBG internal staff (3-4 team members) who process quarterly rebate submissions. No technical knowledge is required.

docs.title: Do I need to install anything?
docs.text: No. The tool runs in your web browser. Just open the URL provided by your IT administrator.

docs.title: What browsers are supported?
docs.text:
• Google Chrome (recommended)
  • Microsoft Edge
  • Firefox

Safari may work but is not officially tested.

docs.title: Is my data secure?
docs.text: Yes. Files are processed on the BBG server and are not stored permanently. After processing and download, cached files are automatically cleaned up.

docs.title: Can I use this tool remotely?
docs.text: If you have access to the tool's URL and are on the BBG network (or VPN), yes. Check with your IT administrator about remote access.

docs.title: What file formats are supported?
docs.text:
The tool accepts: - .xlsm (Excel Macro-Enabled Workbook) - recommended - .xlsx (Excel Workbook)

Old .xls format is not supported. Convert to .xlsm first.

docs.title: What is the maximum file size?
docs.text: 50MB per file. Most rebate files are well under this limit (typically 5-10MB).

docs.title: How many files can I process at once?
docs.text: In batch mode, you can process up to 50 files at once.

docs.title: How long does processing take?
docs.text:
• Single file: 5-30 seconds depending on size
  • Batch (10 files): 30-60 seconds
  • Batch (50 files): 2-5 minutes

docs.title: What's the difference between OLD and NEW format files?
docs.text:
NEW Format: - Cell B6 contains the BBG Member ID - Most recent member submissions use this format

OLD Format: - Cell B6 is blank - Member ID is looked up by member name in cell B7 - Legacy files use this format

The tool detects and handles both automatically. You don't need to do anything different.

docs.title: Can I process multiple files from different members at once?
docs.text: Yes! In batch mode, you can upload files from different members. The tool processes each file according to its member information.

docs.title: What happens if a file has errors?
docs.text:
The tool will show an error message explaining what's wrong. Common issues: - Missing required sheets - Member not found in directory - Invalid file structure

Fix the issue and try again, or check the Troubleshooting Guide.

docs.title: What output format does the tool produce?
docs.text:
The tool generates CSV (Comma-Separated Values) files with exactly 15 columns formatted for TradeNet import:

  1. member_name
  2. bbg_member_id
  3. confirmed_occupancy
  4. job_code
  5. address1
  6. city
  7. state
  8. zip_postal
  9. address_type
  10. quantity
  11. product_id
  12. supplier_name
  13. tradenet_supplier_id
  14. pp_dist_subcontractor
  15. tradenet_company_id

docs.title: How many rows will be in my output?
docs.text:
It depends on the input file. Typical outputs: - Small members: 100-300 rows - Large members: 500-1500 rows per file

The tool "unpivots" the wide Excel format into one row per product per job, so the output has many more rows than the input.

docs.title: What does "unpivot" mean?
docs.text:
Excel files have products across columns (wide format). TradeNet needs one row per product (long format). The tool converts from wide to long automatically.

Example: - Excel: 1 row with 5 products = 1 row - CSV: 1 row with 5 products = 5 rows (one per product)

docs.title: Why are some supplier IDs missing?
docs.text:
If tradenet_supplier_id shows "Not Found" or is blank: - The supplier isn't in the Suppliers lookup table - Add the supplier, then re-process the file

See Lookup Management for details.

docs.title: Can I edit the CSV after downloading?
docs.text: Yes. You can open the CSV in Excel and make manual edits if needed before importing to TradeNet. However, it's better to fix issues at the source (update lookup tables or rules).

docs.title: What is the preview for?
docs.text: The preview lets you verify the data before downloading: - Check member name and ID are correct - Scan through the data for obvious errors - Verify row count makes sense - Confirm supplier mappings look right

docs.title: Are all rows shown in the preview?
docs.text:
Single file mode: Yes, all rows are shown (scrollable table) Batch merged mode: First 200 rows shown; total count is accurate

All data is in the download regardless of preview limitations.

docs.title: What are lookup tables?
docs.text:
Lookup tables are reference directories: - Members Directory: BBG member names and their TradeNet IDs (~911 members) - Suppliers Directory: Supplier names and their TradeNet IDs (~91 suppliers)

These are used to enrich the data during processing.

docs.title: How often should I update lookup tables?
docs.text: Update lookup tables: - Quarterly: When TradeNet provides updated directories - As needed: When new members join or supplier info changes - Before batch processing: Ensure tables are current

docs.title: Can I update just one member or supplier?
docs.text: Yes. Use the "Add" or "Edit" buttons to update individual records without replacing the entire directory.

docs.title: What happens if I upload a new CSV?
docs.text: Uploading a CSV replaces all existing records in that table. Make sure you have a backup of the current data first.

docs.title: Where do I get the lookup CSVs?
docs.text: TradeNet provides updated member and supplier directories periodically. Your administrator should receive these. Keep the most recent versions.

docs.title: What are business rules?
docs.text:
Business rules are IF-THEN logic statements that automatically modify data during processing. Example:

IF supplier_name equals "Day & Night Heating & Cooling"
THEN set supplier_name to "Carrier"

docs.title: Why do we need business rules?
docs.text: Sometimes the supplier name in member files doesn't match what TradeNet expects. Rules automatically fix these discrepancies without manual editing.

docs.title: How many rules can I create?
docs.text: No hard limit, but keep it manageable (typically 10-20 rules). Too many rules become hard to maintain.

docs.title: What happens if I disable a rule?
docs.text: Disabling a rule means it won't be applied during processing, but it's still saved. You can re-enable it later without recreating it.

docs.title: What's the difference between disabling and deleting a rule?
docs.text:
• Disable: Rule is saved but not applied; can be re-enabled later
  • Delete: Rule is permanently removed; must be recreated if needed

Disabling is safer if you're unsure whether you'll need the rule again.

docs.title: Can rules conflict with each other?
docs.text: Yes. If two rules both match the same data, the higher priority rule (lower number) wins. Arrange priorities carefully to avoid conflicts.

docs.title: How do I test a rule?
docs.text:
1. Create the rule
  2. Enable it
  3. Process a test file that should trigger the rule
  4. Check the preview to see if the rule applied correctly
  5. Adjust as needed

docs.title: Who should create or edit rules?
docs.text:
Someone familiar with: - BBG member data - TradeNet supplier requirements - Common data quality issues

Typically a designated team member with management approval.

docs.title: Can I process files offline?
docs.text: No. The tool requires an internet connection to access the server where processing happens.

docs.title: Where are files stored?
docs.text: Files are temporarily stored on the BBG server during processing. After download, cached files are automatically cleaned up. Original files are not permanently stored.

docs.title: What if the server goes down?
docs.text: Contact your IT administrator. They can restart the server or troubleshoot the issue.

docs.title: Can I access the tool from home?
docs.text: Check with your IT administrator about remote access policies. You may need VPN access to reach the tool.

docs.title: Is there an API or command-line version?
docs.text: The tool has a backend API, but it's designed for internal use by the web interface. Contact the development team if you have specific integration needs.

docs.title: Can the tool handle other file types besides rebate files?
docs.text: No. It's specifically designed for BBG quarterly rebate Excel files using the standard template. Other file types will not process correctly.

docs.title: Is the output really ready for TradeNet?
docs.text: Yes. The CSV format matches TradeNet's import specification exactly (15 columns in the correct order with proper formatting).

docs.title: Do I need to edit the CSV before importing?
docs.text: Usually no. However, you may want to: - Review for data quality - Add any missing supplier IDs manually - Fix any member-specific issues

docs.title: What if TradeNet rejects my import?
docs.text: Check the TradeNet error message. Common issues: - Missing supplier IDs (add to lookup table, reprocess) - Invalid member IDs (update lookup table) - Date formatting (shouldn't happen, contact IT if it does)

docs.title: Can I merge multiple CSVs for one TradeNet import?
docs.text: Yes. Use batch mode with "Merged CSV" output option. The tool combines all files into one CSV with all rows from all members.

docs.title: How do I import the CSV to TradeNet?
docs.text: Follow your normal TradeNet import procedures. The tool generates the file; TradeNet import is a separate process handled by your team's workflow.

docs.title: What if a member resubmits a corrected file?
docs.text: Simply process the new file. The tool doesn't track file history, so each processing is independent. Use the new output for your TradeNet import.

docs.title: When should I use batch mode?
docs.text: Use batch mode when you have multiple files to process: - End of quarter with many member submissions - Catching up on backlog - Processing files for the entire quarter at once

docs.title: What's the difference between Merged CSV and ZIP output?
docs.text:
Merged CSV: - All files combined into one large CSV - One import to TradeNet with all data - Faster import process

ZIP Archive: - Individual CSVs for each file - Separate imports to TradeNet - Keeps member data separate

docs.title: Can I preview before downloading in ZIP mode?
docs.text: No. ZIP mode downloads immediately after processing. Use Merged CSV mode if you want to preview before downloading.

docs.title: What if one file fails in a batch?
docs.text: Currently, if any file fails, the entire batch fails. Process files individually to identify and fix problematic files, then reprocess the batch.

docs.title: Can I cancel batch processing mid-way?
docs.text: Refresh the page to cancel, but you'll lose the processing progress. It's better to wait for completion or reduce batch size next time.

docs.title: Scenario: New member joins BBG
docs.text:
What to do: 1. Get member's name and TradeNet ID 2. Go to Lookup Tables → Members 3. Click "Add Member" 4. Fill in name and ID 5. Save

Now you can process files from this member.

docs.title: Scenario: Supplier name changes
docs.text: What to do: 1. Update the Suppliers lookup table with new name 2. OR create a business rule to map old name to new name 3. Reprocess any affected files

docs.title: Scenario: TradeNet updates their member directory
docs.text: What to do: 1. Get the new CSV from TradeNet 2. Go to Lookup Tables → Members 3. Click "Upload CSV" 4. Select the new file 5. Confirm replace

docs.title: Scenario: Need to process 200 files
docs.text: What to do: Process in batches of 50: 1. Enable Batch Mode 2. Select 50 files 3. Choose output format (Merged or ZIP) 4. Process and download 5. Repeat for next 50 files

docs.title: Scenario: Member used wrong template
docs.text: What to do: 1. Try processing - it may still work 2. If it fails, contact the member 3. Request resubmission using correct BBG template 4. Provide them with the standard template if needed

docs.title: For Daily Use
docs.text: ✅ Process test file first before batch processing ✅ Review preview before downloading ✅ Keep lookup tables up to date ✅ Verify member files use correct template

docs.title: For Maintenance
docs.text: ✅ Update lookup tables quarterly ✅ Review business rules monthly ✅ Test with sample files after making changes ✅ Keep TradeNet CSV backups

docs.title: For Troubleshooting
docs.text: ✅ Check error message first ✅ Consult troubleshooting guide ✅ Test with different file to isolate issue ✅ Document unusual cases for team reference

docs.title: Documentation Resources
docs.text:
• User Guide - Complete usage instructions
  • Getting Started - First-time setup
  • Lookup Management - Managing directories
  • Rules Engine - Business rules documentation
  • Troubleshooting - Error solutions

docs.title: Still Have Questions?
docs.text:
1. Check if your question is in this FAQ
  2. Review the relevant documentation guide
  3. Check the troubleshooting guide for errors
  4. Contact your team lead or administrator
  5. Contact IT support with detailed information

docs.title: Can I request a new feature?
docs.text: Yes! Contact your administrator or the development team with: - What feature you need - Why it would be helpful - How often you'd use it - Any examples or details

docs.title: Will the tool get updates?
docs.text:
The tool is actively maintained. Updates may include: - Bug fixes - New features - Performance improvements - Enhanced error handling

Your administrator will notify you of any major updates.

docs.title: Processing Workflow
docs.text:
Single File: 1. Upload file → 2. Process & Preview → 3. Review → 4. Download CSV

Batch Merged: 1. Enable Batch Mode → 2. Select Merged CSV → 3. Upload files → 4. Process & Preview → 5. Download CSV

Batch ZIP: 1. Enable Batch Mode → 2. Select ZIP Archive → 3. Upload files → 4. Process & Download

docs.title: When to Update Lookup Tables
docs.text: When Action New member joins Add to Members table Member name changes Edit Members table New supplier added Add to Suppliers table Quarterly TradeNet update Upload new CSVs

docs.title: When to Use Business Rules
docs.text:
Scenario Rule Type Supplier name doesn't match TradeNet Supplier name equals rule Specific product always one supplier Product ID contains rule Special member-specific mapping Complex rule with AND/OR

Still have questions? Contact your IT administrator or check the other documentation guides.

Builders Buying Group - Rebate Processing Tool

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you access and start using the BBG Rebate Processing Tool for the first time.

docs.title: Accessing the Tool
docs.text:
The tool runs as a web application in your browser. Your IT administrator will provide you with the URL.

Typical URL format: 

http://[server-address]:5174

Simply open this URL in your web browser. We recommend using: - Google Chrome (recommended) - Microsoft Edge - Firefox

docs.title: First Look
docs.text: When you open the tool, you'll see the main interface with three tabs at the top:

docs.title: 1. Upload & Process Tab
docs.text: This is where you'll spend most of your time. Use this tab to upload Excel files and download the processed CSV files.

docs.title: 2. Rules Engine Tab
docs.text: View and manage business rules that control how supplier names are mapped. You'll rarely need to change these unless directed by management.

docs.title: 3. Lookup Tables Tab
docs.text: Manage the member and supplier directories. You may need to update these when new members join or supplier information changes.

docs.title: Your First File Upload
docs.text: Let's process your first rebate file:

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Your File
docs.text: Make sure you have a quarterly usage reporting Excel file (.xlsm format). The file should be the standard BBG rebate template that members submit.

docs.title: Step 2: Go to Upload & Process Tab
docs.text: Click on the Upload & Process tab (should be selected by default).

docs.title: Step 3: Upload Your File
docs.text:
You have two ways to upload:

Option A: Drag and Drop 1. Drag your Excel file from your computer 2. Drop it into the upload area

Option B: Click to Browse 1. Click on the upload area 2. Browse to select your file 3. Click "Open"

docs.title: Step 4: Process the File
docs.text:
1. After the file uploads, click the Process & Preview button
  2. Wait a few seconds while the file is processed
  3. You'll see a loading indicator with a spinner

docs.title: Step 5: Review the Preview
docs.text: Once processing completes, you'll see: - Member name and ID at the top - Total number of rows processed - A scrollable table showing all the data - Summary statistics

docs.title: Step 6: Download Your CSV
docs.text:
1. Review the preview to make sure everything looks correct
  2. Click the Download CSV button
  3. The file will download to your default downloads folder
  4. The filename will be automatically generated based on the original file name

docs.title: Step 7: Import to TradeNet
docs.text: The downloaded CSV is now ready to import directly into TradeNet. Follow your normal TradeNet import procedures.

docs.title: Processing Multiple Files (Batch Mode)
docs.text: If you have several files to process at once:

docs.title: Step 1: Enable Batch Mode
docs.text:
1. Check the Batch Mode checkbox above the upload area
  2. Choose your output format:
  3. Merged CSV: All files combined into one CSV
  4. ZIP Archive: Individual CSVs bundled in a ZIP file

docs.title: Step 2: Select Multiple Files
docs.text:
1. Click the upload area
  2. Hold Ctrl (Windows) or Cmd (Mac) to select multiple files
  3. Or drag and drop multiple files at once
  4. Maximum 50 files per batch

docs.title: Step 3: Process Batch
docs.text:
1. Click Process & Preview [X] Files (for merged) or Process & Download [X] Files (for ZIP)
  2. Wait for processing to complete
  3. For merged CSV, review the preview before downloading
  4. For ZIP mode, the file downloads automatically

docs.title: Color Codes
docs.text:
• Blue: Primary actions and active elements
  • Green: Success messages and completed actions
  • Red: Errors and warnings
  • Yellow: Warnings that don't prevent processing

docs.title: Status Messages
docs.text:
• Processing...: The system is working on your file
  • Processing Complete!: Success, your file is ready
  • Processing Error: Something went wrong (see error message for details)

docs.title: Buttons
docs.text:
• Process & Preview: Analyze the file and show results
  • Download CSV: Save the processed file to your computer
  • Process Another File: Start over with a new file

docs.title: Browser
docs.text:
• Modern web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox)
  • JavaScript enabled
  • Cookies enabled

docs.title: Files
docs.text:
• Excel files in .xlsm or .xlsx format
  • Maximum file size: 50MB
  • Standard BBG rebate template format

docs.title: Internet Connection
docs.text:
• Required to access the tool
  • Stable connection recommended during file processing

docs.title: What Happens to My Files?
docs.text:
When you upload a file: 1. It's temporarily stored on the server for processing 2. The data is extracted and transformed 3. The CSV is generated 4. After download, cached files are automatically cleaned up

Your files are not permanently stored on the server. They exist only during the processing session.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you know the basics:

  • Read the User Guide for detailed instructions on all features
  • Learn about Lookup Management if you need to update member or supplier directories
  • Review the Rules Engine to understand how business rules work
  • Check the Troubleshooting Guide if you run into issues

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
• Check the FAQ for common questions
  • Review the Troubleshooting Guide for error solutions
  • Contact your IT administrator
  • Email the development team

Ready to start? Head to the User Guide for complete usage instructions.

docs.title: Lookup Management
docs.text: This guide explains how to manage the lookup tables that power the BBG Rebate Processing Tool.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• What Are Lookup Tables?
  • Accessing Lookup Tables
  • Members Directory
  • Suppliers Directory
  • Uploading New Directories
  • Adding Individual Records
  • Editing Records
  • Deleting Records
  • Best Practices

docs.title: What Are Lookup Tables?
docs.text: Lookup tables are reference directories that contain critical information used during file processing:

docs.title: Members Directory
docs.text:
Contains: BBG member companies and their TradeNet IDs Used for: - Looking up Member IDs when processing OLD format files - Validating member information - Enriching output data with correct TradeNet IDs

Current size: ~911 members

docs.title: Suppliers Directory
docs.text:
Contains: Supplier companies and their TradeNet IDs Used for: - Mapping supplier names to TradeNet Supplier IDs - Validating supplier information - Ensuring correct supplier attribution in output

Current size: ~91 suppliers

docs.title: Why They Matter
docs.text:
Without accurate lookup tables: - OLD format files won't process (can't find Member ID) - Supplier IDs will be missing or incorrect - TradeNet imports will fail

Keep these tables up to date!

docs.title: Step 1: Navigate to Lookup Tables Tab
docs.text:
Click the Lookup Tables tab at the top of the page.

[Screenshot: Lookup Tables tab in main navigation]

docs.title: Step 2: Choose Members or Suppliers
docs.text:
You'll see two sub-tabs: - Members - View and manage the Members directory - Suppliers - View and manage the Suppliers directory

[Screenshot: Members and Suppliers sub-tabs]

docs.title: Viewing Members
docs.text:
The Members table displays: - Member Name: Company name (e.g., "Bosgraaf Homes") - BBG Member ID: TradeNet member ID (e.g., "1003") - Actions: Edit and Delete buttons

[Screenshot: Members table with sample data]

docs.title: Table Features
docs.text: Search: Type in the search box to filter by name or ID Sort: Click column headers to sort Pagination: Navigate through pages if you have many records

docs.title: Members Data Format
docs.text:
Each member record contains: - member_name: Full company name - bbg_member_id: TradeNet member ID (usually 4 digits)

Example: 

member_name: "Bosgraaf Homes"
bbg_member_id: "1003"

docs.title: When to Update Members
docs.text: Update the Members directory when: - A new member joins BBG - A member's name changes - A member's TradeNet ID changes - TradeNet provides an updated member list

docs.title: Viewing Suppliers
docs.text:
The Suppliers table displays: - Supplier Name: Company name (e.g., "Carrier") - TradeNet Supplier ID: TradeNet ID (e.g., "5001") - Actions: Edit and Delete buttons

[Screenshot: Suppliers table with sample data]

docs.title: Table Features
docs.text: Search: Type in the search box to filter by name or ID Sort: Click column headers to sort Pagination: Navigate through pages if you have many records

docs.title: Suppliers Data Format
docs.text:
Each supplier record contains: - supplier_name: Full company name as it appears in TradeNet - tradenet_supplier_id: TradeNet supplier ID

Example: 

supplier_name: "Carrier"
tradenet_supplier_id: "5001"

docs.title: When to Update Suppliers
docs.text: Update the Suppliers directory when: - A new supplier is added to TradeNet - A supplier's name changes - A supplier's TradeNet ID changes - TradeNet provides an updated supplier directory

docs.title: Uploading New Directories
docs.text: The fastest way to update lookup tables is to upload a complete CSV file from TradeNet.

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Your CSV
docs.text:
Get the latest Members directory from TradeNet as a CSV file.

Required CSV format: 

member_name,bbg_member_id
Bosgraaf Homes,1003
New Tradition Homes,1564
Example Builders,1234

Requirements: - First row must be headers: member_name,bbg_member_id - Each subsequent row is one member - No extra columns (will be ignored)

docs.title: Step 2: Upload
docs.text:
1. Go to Lookup Tables → Members tab
  2. Click the Upload CSV button
  3. Select your members CSV file
  4. Click "Open"

[Screenshot: Upload CSV button]

docs.title: Step 3: Confirm Replace
docs.text:
You'll see a confirmation dialog:

"This will replace all existing members with the contents of this file. Are you sure?"

  • Click Confirm to proceed
  • Click Cancel to abort

docs.title: Step 4: Verification
docs.text:
After upload: - You'll see a success message - The table will refresh with the new data - Verify a few records to ensure data loaded correctly

[Screenshot: Success message after upload]

docs.title: Uploading Suppliers CSV
docs.text:
Same process as Members, but for suppliers:

Required CSV format: 

supplier_name,tradenet_supplier_id
Carrier,5001
CertainTeed,5002
Air Vent,5003

Requirements: - First row must be headers: supplier_name,tradenet_supplier_id - Each subsequent row is one supplier

docs.title: Upload Safety
docs.text: Important Notes: - Uploading a CSV replaces all existing records - There is no undo - make sure you have a backup - Test with a small file first if unsure - Keep a copy of the current data before replacing

docs.title: Adding Individual Records
docs.text: Add a single member or supplier without uploading a full CSV.

docs.title: Step 1: Open Add Form
docs.text:
1. Go to Lookup Tables → Members
  2. Click the Add Member button

[Screenshot: Add Member button]

docs.title: Step 2: Fill in the Form
docs.text:
Enter the required information: - Member Name: Full company name - BBG Member ID: TradeNet member ID (4-5 digits usually)

[Screenshot: Add Member form]

docs.title: Step 3: Save
docs.text:
1. Click Save
  2. The new member appears in the table
  3. Success message confirms the addition

docs.title: Adding a Supplier
docs.text: Same process as adding a member:

docs.title: Step 1: Open Add Form
docs.text:
1. Go to Lookup Tables → Suppliers
  2. Click the Add Supplier button

docs.title: Step 2: Fill in the Form
docs.text: Enter: - Supplier Name: Full company name as it appears in TradeNet - TradeNet Supplier ID: Supplier ID from TradeNet

docs.title: Step 3: Save
docs.text: Click Save to add the supplier.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: The form validates: - Required fields must be filled - IDs must be valid format - Names cannot be duplicates (warning shown)

docs.title: Editing Records
docs.text: Update an existing member or supplier record.

docs.title: Step 1: Find the Record
docs.text:
1. Go to Lookup Tables → Members
  2. Use search or scroll to find the member
  3. Click the Edit button in that row

[Screenshot: Edit button in member row]

docs.title: Step 2: Update the Form
docs.text:
The edit form appears with current values filled in: - Modify Member Name and/or BBG Member ID - Make your changes

[Screenshot: Edit Member form with existing values]

docs.title: Step 3: Save Changes
docs.text:
1. Click Save
  2. The table updates with new values
  3. Success message confirms the update

docs.title: Editing a Supplier
docs.text: Same process: 1. Go to Lookup Tables → Suppliers 2. Find the supplier 3. Click Edit 4. Update values 5. Click Save

docs.title: When to Edit
docs.text: Edit records when: - A company name changes (merger, rebranding) - An ID changes in TradeNet - You discover a typo or error - TradeNet notifies you of changes

docs.title: Deleting Records
docs.text: Remove a member or supplier from the directory.

docs.title: Step 1: Find the Record
docs.text:
1. Go to Lookup Tables → Members
  2. Find the member to delete
  3. Click the Delete button in that row

[Screenshot: Delete button in member row]

docs.title: Step 2: Confirm Deletion
docs.text:
A confirmation dialog appears:

"Are you sure you want to delete this member?"

  • Click Confirm to delete
  • Click Cancel to abort

docs.title: Step 3: Verification
docs.text:
• The record is removed from the table
  • Success message confirms deletion

docs.title: Deleting a Supplier
docs.text: Same process: 1. Go to Lookup Tables → Suppliers 2. Find the supplier 3. Click Delete 4. Confirm

docs.title: When to Delete
docs.text:
Delete records when: - A member leaves BBG - A supplier is no longer used - Duplicate records exist - Records are obsolete

Warning: Deleting a member may cause OLD format files from that member to fail processing.

docs.title: Regular Maintenance
docs.text: ✅ Update quarterly: Check for updates from TradeNet each quarter ✅ Before batch processing: Ensure directories are current ✅ After TradeNet changes: Update immediately if TradeNet notifies you of changes

docs.title: Backup Strategy
docs.text: ✅ Export current data: Before uploading new CSVs, export the current tables ✅ Keep TradeNet originals: Save the original CSV files from TradeNet ✅ Document changes: Keep a log of manual edits

docs.title: Data Quality
docs.text: ✅ Consistent naming: Use exact company names as they appear in TradeNet ✅ Verify IDs: Double-check IDs match TradeNet exactly ✅ Test after changes: Process a sample file after major updates ✅ No duplicates: Check for duplicate entries regularly

docs.title: Troubleshooting Lookup Issues
docs.text:
Problem: File fails with "Member not found" Solution: Add the member to the Members directory

Problem: Supplier ID missing in output Solution: Check if supplier exists in Suppliers directory, add if missing

Problem: Wrong supplier ID in output Solution: Edit the supplier record or check business rules

docs.title: Access Control
docs.text:
Who should update lookup tables? - Designated staff member(s) with TradeNet access - Someone who receives TradeNet directory updates - Authorized by management

Coordination: - Notify team when you update directories - Keep a change log for reference - Don't let multiple people update simultaneously

docs.title: Members CSV Template
docs.text:
member_name,bbg_member_id
Bosgraaf Homes,1003
New Tradition Homes,1564
ABC Builders,1100
XYZ Construction,1200

Rules: - Header row required - Two columns exactly - No blank rows - No extra columns (ignored if present) - UTF-8 encoding recommended

docs.title: Suppliers CSV Template
docs.text:
supplier_name,tradenet_supplier_id
Carrier,5001
CertainTeed,5002
Air Vent,5003
Heatilator,5004
Leading Edge,5005

Rules: - Header row required - Two columns exactly - No blank rows - No extra columns (ignored if present) - UTF-8 encoding recommended

docs.title: Update Workflow
docs.text:
Full Directory Update: 1. Get latest CSV from TradeNet 2. Go to Lookup Tables → [Members/Suppliers] 3. Click Upload CSV 4. Select file 5. Confirm replace 6. Verify data loaded

Single Record Update: 1. Go to Lookup Tables → [Members/Suppliers] 2. Click Add or Edit 3. Fill in form 4. Click Save 5. Verify in table

docs.title: Common Tasks
